topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the library hummed with an aggressive monotony, mirroring the tightness in my chest. I stared at the printed accusation, the ink seeming to swim before my eyes. "This is a complete fabrication," I muttered, the sound barely audible over the shuffling of nearby pages. My hands clenched into fists, the knuckles turning white against the worn cover of my textbook. Every word they had written felt like a deliberate assault on everything I had poured into that paper. I could feel a heat rising in my neck, a prickly sensation that made the hairs on my arms stand up. I pushed my chair back with a scrape that echoed too loudly in the quiet aisle, the sudden movement making several students look up, and I fixed my gaze on the professor, my jaw set so hard I could feel the muscle tremor.

[story 2]

"You can't be serious," I snapped, my voice sharp enough to cut through the hushed reverence of the seminar room. I felt a sudden, dizzying rush, like the air had been knocked out of my lungs. My palms were slick with cold sweat as I gripped the edge of the desk, my breath catching in short, shallow gasps. The evidence laid out on the lectern seemed flimsy, a ridiculous collection of highlighted passages that didn't reflect the genuine struggle and late nights I had invested. I wanted to shout, to tear the paper in half, but I forced myself to breathe, focusing instead on the furious drumming beneath my ribs. I looked directly at the committee members, my eyes blazing with a raw, uncontained frustration that made my vision swim momentarily.

[story 3]

I paced the small confines of my dorm room, the floorboards protesting under my agitated stride. Each step was heavy, deliberate, fueled by a simmering resentment that had been building for weeks. I stopped abruptly, spinning around to face the wall, my chest heaving. The accusation sat on my desk, mocking me with its neat, damning structure. A low growl escaped my throat, a sound ragged with suppressed anger. I snatched the document up, my fingers trembling slightly, and then, with a sudden, decisive movement, I crumpled the paper into a tight ball before throwing it toward the wastebasket. The sound of the paper hitting the bin was the only release, a small, violent expulsion of the tension coiled tight in my gut.
